# TN — The agent transaction protocol

> A library for writing signed, encrypted, append-only log entries
> per business event. Every action, a TransactioN. Python and
> TypeScript bindings share a byte-identical wire format.

Install: `pip install tn-proto`. Source:
`https://github.com/cyaxios/tn-proto`.

## Mental model

- A **ceremony** is one (publisher, ciphers, recipients) tuple.
  State lives on disk under `.tn/<ceremony>/`.
- An **entry** is one signed, encrypted protocol envelope. Every
  `tn.info` / `tn.log` call writes exactly one envelope.
- A **stream** is a named ceremony layered on a shared project
  identity (via `extends:` in tn.yaml). All streams under a project
  sign with the same DID.
- A **group** is a cipher domain inside a ceremony. Each event
  routes into one or more groups based on yaml field rules. Each
  group has its own readers.
- A **kit** is cipher-specific per-group reader material. A BTN publisher
  mints reader kits; a JWE reader owns its `.jwe.mykey` and enrolls the
  authenticated public key; a HIBE authority grants a path capability.
- A **`.tnpkg`** is a signed zip wrapper for moving state between
  participants: bundles, snapshots, invites, identity seeds.

## The five verbs

- `tn.init(name=None, *, profile="transaction", ...)` — resolve or
  create a ceremony and bind the runtime. `tn.init()` with no args
  resolves `default`.
- `tn.info / tn.warning / tn.error / tn.debug` — write one signed
  encrypted envelope at that level. Threshold-aware: silenced when
  below `tn.set_level`.
- `tn.log(event_type, *, level="", **fields)` — severity-less write
  (default `level=""`). Ignores threshold. Use this for custom
  levels (`"trace"`, `"audit"`) or events that must survive the
  filter. Not an alias for `tn.info`.
- `tn.read(...)` — iterate decoded entries from disk. Default
  `all_runs=True` (every entry on disk).
- `tn.watch(...)` — tail the log live (async iterator).

`tn.absorb(path)` and `tn.export(path, kind=...)` move `.tnpkg`
bundles in and out. `tn.pkg.bundle_for_recipient(did, path)` is
BTN-only: it mints and bundles a BTN kit. JWE uses a reader-owned
`.jwe.mykey` plus authenticated public-key enrollment; HIBE uses
`tn.admin.grant_reader`.

## Where state lives

- **Per-user identity** (device DID + signing key, one per human or
  machine): `~/.local/share/tn/identity.json` on POSIX,
  `%APPDATA%\tn\identity.json` on Windows. Override with
  `TN_IDENTITY_DIR`.
- **Per-project ceremony state**: `<project>/.tn/<ceremony>/`,
  containing `tn.yaml`, `keys/`, `logs/`, `admin/`, optional
  `vault/` cache.
- **Vault state** (when linked): cached locally under the ceremony
  dir, plus the remote vault project at
  `https://vault.tn-proto.org` or a self-hosted instance.

## Vault: linked by default, network-quiet by default

Fresh ceremonies are minted `mode: linked` against
`https://vault.tn-proto.org`. Nothing reaches the network until an
explicit vault verb fires (`tn wallet link`, `tn wallet restore`,
etc.). To opt out at init: `tn.init(link=False)` or env var
`TN_NO_LINK=1`. For fully air-gapped, set `ceremony.mode: local`
in `tn.yaml`.

Vault auth is DID challenge / verify: the client signs a server
nonce with its Ed25519 device key; the vault returns a JWT.
Implementation: `python/tn/vault_client.py:164` and
`python/tn/bootstrap.py:144`.

## Container and CI bootstrap

A container has no home directory and no on-disk keystore.
Cold-start uses the `TN_API_KEY` environment variable:

1. Mint a persistent key at
   `https://vault.tn-proto.org/account` (Projects → API keys →
   Generate persistent key). Format:
   `tn_apikey_<43-char>_<22-char>`.
2. Plant the bearer in the platform's secret store as env var
   `TN_API_KEY`. Cloudflare Secrets Store, GitHub Actions secrets,
   AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, Azure Key Vault, plain
   Docker `-e` — all work the same way at the consumer.
3. Deploy. On first boot, `tn.bootstrap.bootstrap_from_api_key`
   exchanges the bearer for the sealed keystore via the vault's
   `/api/v1/api-keys/{key_id}/sealed-bundle` endpoint and writes it
   to disk. Subsequent boots reuse the on-disk cache.

**Precedence: disk wins over env.** If
`<keystore>/local.private` already exists, TN ignores `TN_API_KEY`
and uses the on-disk keystore. To force re-bootstrap, delete the
keystore directory.

Implementation: `python/tn/handlers/registry.py:454` for the gate,
`python/tn/bootstrap.py` for the full flow.

## Cipher modes

- `btn` — NNL subset-difference broadcast tree. Sub-millisecond
  encrypt for thousands of readers; selective revocation without
  rekeying survivors. The default.
- `jwe` — standards-defined JOSE for small explicit audiences. It uses
  one fresh CEK to seal one shared encrypted body, with one wrapped-CEK
  recipient block per reader.
- `hibe` — evaluation-only identity-path encryption under an authority
  public key. Writers can seal before the reader key exists, and the
  authority later grants path keys. The `tn-bbg` scheme implementation
  and `bls12_381_plus` pairing library are unaudited; external
  cryptographic review is required before production use.

All three modes speak the same on-disk wire format. Choose per-group in
`tn.yaml`. Encryption is always on; the protocol floor.

## Profiles

`tn.init(profile=...)` picks a curated bundle:

| profile     | encrypts | signs | chains | flush    | default sink   |
|-------------|----------|-------|--------|----------|----------------|
| transaction | yes      | yes   | yes    | fsync    | file_rotating  |
| audit       | yes      | yes   | yes    | buffered | file_rotating  |
| secure_log  | yes      | yes   | no     | buffered | file_rotating  |
| telemetry   | yes      | no    | no     | async    | stdout         |
| stdout      | yes      | no    | no     | async    | stdout         |

Profiles are SDK-fixed, not user-composable. Pick the closest
match and override individual axes in `tn.yaml` only when needed.

## `.tnpkg` shipping format

Universal signed-zip wrapper for moving TN state between machines.
Manifest is Ed25519-signed by `publisher_identity`. Kinds in v1:

- `admin_log_snapshot` — chain catch-up
- `offer` / `enrolment` / `recipient_invite` — JWE bootstrap
- `kit_bundle` — one reader's key material
- `full_keystore` — entire keystore including secrets; opt-in
- `identity_seed` — minimal "this is who I am" bundle, self-signed
- `contact_update` — vault-emitted inbox event

Verbs: `tn.export(path, kind=...)`, `tn.absorb(path)`. The
`tn.pkg.bundle_for_recipient(did, path)` helper is BTN-only. JWE uses a
reader-owned `.jwe.mykey` plus authenticated public-key enrollment; HIBE
uses `tn.admin.grant_reader`.

## CLI

- `tn init [./project]` — provision identity + ceremony
- `tn add_recipient <group> <name>` — mint a kit, wrap as `.tnpkg`
- `tn rotate` — rotate group keys; emit per-recipient `.tnpkg`
- `tn absorb <pkg>` — install someone's bundle
- `tn read [--all-runs]` — decoded entries to stdout
- `tn wallet status|link|restore` — vault operations
- `tn validate` — validate the project's config tree
- `tn streams` — list ceremonies under `.tn/`

Non-interactive by default; safe in CI and containers. A TTY
enables prompts where useful.

## Cross-language parity

| binding         | install                    | use case                       |
|-----------------|----------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Python          | `pip install tn-proto`  | reference, CLI, services       |
| TypeScript/Node | `tn-proto` (npm)            | Node services, build tools     |
| Browser (WASM)  | bundled via `tn-proto-web` | in-browser verify and decrypt  |

Every binding reads byte-identical envelopes. Cross-language parity
is enforced by CI on every PR.

## Key paths into the source

- `python/tn/__init__.py` — public surface
- `python/tn/identity.py` — per-user identity, BIP-39, HKDF chain
- `python/tn/_layout.py` — on-disk layout rules for `.tn/<name>/`
- `python/tn/bootstrap.py` — `TN_API_KEY` cold-start
- `python/tn/vault_client.py` — vault HTTP client (DID auth, sync,
  restore)
- `python/tn/wallet.py` — link, sync, restore ceremonies
- `python/tn/tnpkg.py` — manifest schema, signed-zip wire format
- `python/tn/btn_keystore.py` — on-disk btn rotation layout
- `python/tn/_keystore_backend.py` — atomic write + CAS + lock
